title: Emit security audit findings into the priority hub
description: Make audit results visible in the unified priority surface: after an audit completes (pipeline gate or standalone CLI), critical and important findings are mapped to the canonical hub Finding shape (FindingSchema imported from src/hub/finding.ts, never redefined) and ingested via the existing finding-store path, gated by security.hub (default true within the opt-in security section). Duplicate emission across retries is absorbed by the hub's existing content-hash dedup.

successCriteria.description: A pure exported mapping function converts a SecurityAuditResult into hub Finding objects (one per critical/important finding) that validate against the canonical FindingSchema imported from src/hub/finding.ts, with a severity mapping documented in code (critical bucket → the hub's highest applicable severity; important → a mid severity) and title/body carrying vulnClass, path:line, and description.

When an audit completes with findings and security.hub is true (default), the Findings are ingested via the existing finding-store ingest function through an injected sink (research-runner precedent); with security.hub:false, zero hub writes occur for an otherwise identical audit; a hub ingest failure is caught and logged and never alters the audit verdict or blocks the pipeline/CLI.
Emitting the same audit result twice produces no duplicate hub entries (the hub's existing content-hash dedup absorbs it), demonstrated with the real finding-store in a temp directory.
FindingSchema and the ingest path are imported from src/hub/ — grep confirms no redefinition of the Finding shape and no schema drift; clean audits (no critical/important findings) emit nothing.
npm run build and npm run typecheck pass; ESLint zero errors; full suite green — existing hub tests unchanged.

nonGoals: Do not surface findings in Telegram (out of scope per spec).
Do not modify src/hub/ (FindingSchema, finding-store, priority logic) — consume only.
Do not emit info/minor buckets or scanner-only priors that the LLM auditor did not confirm into the review buckets.
Do not make hub emission affect the gate verdict, exit codes, or timing (fire-and-forget with caught errors, after the verdict is computed).
stopConditions: All five criteria verified with the dedup test running against the REAL finding-store in a temp dir.
definitionOfDone: Security findings from any audit appear as canonical Findings in the priority hub (deduped, severity-mapped, source-attributed), silenced by security.hub:false, and a hub failure can never change an audit outcome.
assumptions: src/hub/finding.ts owns the canonical Zod FindingSchema and src/hub/ exposes an ingest function (research runner binds findingSink→ingestFinding at finding-store.ts:140 per progress notes); the exact severity enum and required fields are read from the actual schema at implementation time — the mapping documented in code must use REAL enum values, not invented ones.
The hub dedup key is content-derived (sha256 over domain|title|kind per prior art), so stable titles per finding (vulnClass + path:line) give retry-idempotent emission.

A 'security' (or closest existing) domain/kind tag is representable in FindingSchema; if the schema has a closed domain enum without a fitting value, use the closest existing value and record the limitation in code comments and the sprint handoff rather than editing the hub schema.
Emission call sites are the audit completion points in runSecurityAudit or its two callers — one shared helper, invoked after verdict computation.
outOfScope: Telegram surfaces, hub schema changes, digest/priority re-ranking, skill/dogfood/docs (sprint 7).
generatorNotes: Anchors may have drifted — re-locate by pattern. (1) READ src/hub/finding.ts and src/hub/finding-store.ts first; mirror how src/research/runner.ts imports FindingSchema/Finding type and takes an INJECTED findingSink dependency (so tests pass a spy and the CLI/pipeline bind ingestFinding at the boundary) — do not import finding-store deep into the auditor core if the research precedent injects instead. (2) New src/orchestrator/security-hub.ts (or a section in security-auditor-agent.ts if tiny): export mapAuditToFindings(result: SecurityAuditResult): Finding[] (pure) and emitSecurityFindings(result, sink, logger) (try/catch, logs failures, returns void). (3) Wire emission after verdict computation in the shared core path so gate and CLI both get it; gate/CLI must not await-fail on it — catch inside emitSecurityFindings. (4) Respect config.security.hub with the ?? true default semantics from the schema; when config.security is absent entirely (standalone CLI default-parse case) the parsed section carries hub:true — fine, the CLI explicitly opted in by running. (5) Dedup test: use the real ingest in a temp .bober; emit twice; count stored findings.
evaluatorNotes: For sc-6-1: validate every mapped object with the REAL FindingSchema.parse (not a hand-rolled check); verify severity mapping uses actual enum members by reading src/hub/finding.ts yourself and cross-checking the code's mapping table. For sc-6-2: three tests — hub:true ingests N findings; hub:false ingests zero; sink that throws → verdict/exit unchanged and a logged warning. For sc-6-3: real finding-store in temp dir, emit twice, assert store count equals first-emission count. For sc-6-4: grep 'z.object' in the new files for any Finding-shaped redefinition; grep imports to confirm they point at src/hub/. Confirm clean-audit no-op (sink zero calls). Full suite green including hub tests.

iterationHistory.reason: sc-6-2: unguarded default-sink store setup rejects on fs failure, flips clean audit to blocked
